world.title: 40 species, including snowy owl, added to UN list for greater protections

science.description: Scientists call for a major acceleration in coral assisted evolution research to help reefs cope with rapidly warming oceans. The study was led by Dr Adriana Humanes, Newcastle University and Dr Juan Ortiz, Australian Institute of Marine Science (AIMS). It highlights fundamental changes needed to generate knowledge fast enough to make these methods effective. The...

science.title: Strained liquid crystals steer soliton 'bullets' along two diagonal paths
science.description: In physics, some waves behave in a surprising way: instead of spreading out and fading, they hold their shape as they travel at constant speeds. These unusual waves, called solitons, have interested scientists since they were first observed in canals in the 19th century. Today, researchers study solitons in everything from optical fibers to biological systems.

science.title: Ancient Fossils from Southern China Reveal Startling New Clues About Life’s Origins
science.description: A new study debunks previous claims that ancient embryo-like fossils from China are remnants of early animal life, revealing they belong to an entirely different class of organisms.

science.description: From color-changing skin to jet-propelled motion, squid and cuttlefish have long fascinated scientists. However, the limited fossil record and incomplete genomic information have made it impossible to confidently order the evolution of these enigmatic creatures, until now....

science.title: How plants could betray themselves across the galaxy
science.description: Here's a thought experiment. Imagine looking at Earth from a distant star system, armed with a powerful telescope capable of capturing its reflected light. Could you tell the planet was alive? The answer, remarkably, might be yes and the clue would come from the color of the plants.
